description: One-pass review of crate secretx-file. Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Decision (autonomous, headless mode): not calling 'question' tool per /review-rusty-crate rules. Reviewing secretx-file/src/lib.rs (357 lines) + Cargo.toml + README.md. cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo test passes 11+1 doctests; rustdoc clean. Pass scope: filesystem backend with atomic temp-then-rename put() and dotdot-rejection on URI parse.
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — durability gap (no fsync) and silent runtime failure on path with no file_name are the two real bugs; the rest is defensible polish on a small (357-line) crate with clean clippy/test/doc baselines.
Correctness confidence: 80% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: behavior on bind-mounted parent directories, cross-process tempfile race when PID has wrapped on long-running systems (extremely unlikely with create_new), exact semantics of std::fs::read on character devices vs. named pipes (qth2.3). Uncertainty: no integration tests against an actual filesystem under crash conditions; no fuzzing on URI parsing (handled in core but file backend specifics not fuzzed).
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — a serious Rust practitioner reads this without wincing; the structure (async_trait + spawn_blocking for sync IO + Zeroizing-protected secret bytes) is the standard idiom for this class of problem. The PathBuf-vs-Arc<Path> choice is the only real ownership smell (qth2.16); everything else is taste-tier or already covered by my-oss findings.
Rust idiom confidence: 90% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: qth2.17 (BackendRegistration coercion) is partially a core-API design issue, not file-specific. Uncertainty: did not study how inventory's submit! macro hygiene composes with the closure types; if that produces non-idiomatic generated code it is invisible to a source-level review.
title: Review: secretx-file rusty pass
